Change 1: Replace:
  # Build clauses for all triples
      clauses = [clause(t.predicate, t.object) for t in triples]
  
      # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
      if len(clauses) == 1:
          sentence = f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}."
      else:
          # commas between all but the last clause, with an Oxford comma
          sentence = f"{main_subject} " + ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + f", and {clauses[-1]}."
      return sentence
with:
  # Helper to format numbers with commas and remove trailing .0
      def _fmt_num(val: str) -> str:
          try:
              # try integer first
              i = int(float(val))
              return f"{i:,}"
          except Exception:
              return val
  
      # Build a fluent sentence, merging consecutive triples when the object of one
      # is the subject of the next (e.g., location → country)
      parts = []
      i = 0
      while i < len(triples):
          cur = triples[i]
          cur_obj = cur.object.strip().strip('"')
          cur_clause = clause(cur.predicate, cur.object)
  
          # Look ahead to see if we can chain with a relative clause
          if i + 1 < len(triples):
              nxt = triples[i + 1]
              if cur_obj == nxt.subject:
                  # Combine with a relative “which …” clause
                  nxt_clause = clause(nxt.predicate, nxt.object)
                  cur_clause = f"{cur_clause}, which {nxt_clause}"
                  i += 1  # skip the next triple because it is merged
  
          parts.append(cur_clause)
          i += 1
